title: Internal combustion engine with integrated connectivity device
abstract: A power tool is provided. The power tool includes an internal combustion engine and an integrated device coupled to the internal combustion engine. The internal combustion engine includes a flywheel or another rotating component and an ignition coil assembly. The integrated device is enclosed in a housing and is coupled adjacent to either the flywheel or a rotating component. The integrated device includes a printed circuit board with a wireless communications module and a power generation portion which receives power wirelessly from the internal combustion engine.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A power tool comprising: an internal combustion engine comprising a flywheel with a magnetic portion and an ignition coil assembly; an integrated device comprising a printed circuit board with a power generation portion enclosed in a housing; and the housing is secured in close proximity to the flywheel such that the integrated device generates power wirelessly from the flywheel when the internal combustion engine is operating. 2. A power tool in accordance with Claim 1 wherein the integrated device is not primarily powered by a battery. 3. A power tool in accordance with Claim 1 wherein the integrated device comprises at least one of: a sensor for at least one of measuring acceleration, measuring angular displacement, and measuring angular acceleration; a microcontroller for at least one of measuring operating time, measuring engine speed, interpreting sensor data, storing operating time, storing operating data, and maintaining a log of operating hours; and a wireless communications module for broadcasting at least one operating data and usage data gathered from the internal combustion engine. 4. A power tool in accordance with Claim 1 wherein the integrated device includes a pole piece to direct and concentrate the magnetic field passing through the power generation portion. 5. A power tool in accordance with Claim 4 wherein the housing conforms to and secures the pole piece in a position that is adjacent to the flywheel. 6. A power tool in accordance with Claim 4 wherein the printed circuit board includes an opening for inserting the pole piece. 7. A power tool in accordance with Claim 6 wherein the opening includes a bushing. 8. A power tool in accordance with Claim 1 wherein the housing is attached to a cover of the internal combustion engine without the use of mechanical fasteners. 9. A power tool in accordance with Claim 8 wherein the housing is attached to the cover with at least one of: a snap-fit; an interference fit; an interlocking connection. 10. A power tool in accordance with Claim 8 wherein the housing is attached to an inside surface of the cover using mechanical fasteners. 11. An engine assembly comprising: an internal combustion engine comprising a flywheel with a magnetic portion and an ignition coil assembly; an integrated device capable of receiving power wirelessly from the internal combustion engine when the flywheel is rotating; and the integrated device is secured in a housing that is attached to the engine assembly. 12. An engine assembly in accordance with Claim 11 wherein the integrated device includes a printed circuit board comprising: a power generation portion configured to harvest energy from movement of the flywheel; a power conditioner configured to maintain a useable voltage for the integrated device as the flywheel rotates; a microcontroller for at least one of measuring operating time, measuring engine speed, interpreting sensor data, storing operating time, storing operating data, and maintaining a log of operating hours; and 

 a wireless communications module configured to broadcast at least one of operating data and usage data. 13. An engine assembly in accordance with Claim 11 wherein the integrated device is not primarily powered by a battery. 14. An engine assembly in accordance with Claim 11 wherein the integrated device includes a pole piece. 15. An engine assembly in accordance with Claim 14 wherein the housing secures the pole piece mounted to the integrated device. 16. An engine assembly in accordance with Claim 11 wherein the housing is attached to a cover of the internal combustion engine. 17. An engine assembly in accordance with Claim 16 wherein the housing is attached to the cover with at least one of: a snap-fit; an interference fit; an interlocking connection; at least one mechanical fastener. 18. A power tool comprising: an internal combustion engine assembly comprising a rotating component and a source of a magnetic field; and an integrated device comprising a printed circuit board with a power generation portion capable of receiving power wirelessly from the source of the magnetic field; and the integrated device is enclosed in a housing with a removable portion. 19. A power tool in accordance with Claim 18 wherein the housing is coupled to a cover with a dovetail joint and a snap-fit. 20. A power tool in accordance with Claim 18 wherein the housing is coupled to a cover with at least one mechanical fastener.
